fixtures.notes: Placement queries return early without caching when the requested rotation is excluded by the config.
A hole-source duplicate must not erase a valid non-hole placement at the same translation when part-in-part placement is disabled.
A feasible bin IFP query produces ranked candidates and the repeated request is served from the placement cache.
Changing the piece geometry revision must invalidate the placement cache key even when all other request fields stay the same.
Hole placements are rejected when part-in-part placement is disabled even if a hole IFP yields an exact fit point.
Placement queries discard candidates whose resolved rotation violates the requested part-to-bed grain relationship even when the discrete rotation index itself is valid.
Explicit keep-outs remove otherwise feasible IFP candidates while leaving non-overlapping placements available for ranking.
Enabling part-in-part placement allows hole IFP candidates to survive filtering and rank like any other placement.
Bottom-left scoring ranks by the smallest y and then smallest x among feasible candidates.
Minimum-length scoring minimizes the resulting occupied width after placing the piece.
Maximum-utilization scoring chooses the candidate that maximizes occupied area divided by convex hull area.
With zero clearance, exact-touch placements remain feasible.
Positive part clearance removes candidates that would sit too close to an occupied boundary.
